resposta:
solution: ```class Solution:
    def canMakeSubsequence(self, str1: str, str2: str) -> bool:
        if len(str2) > len(str1):
            return False
        i = j = 0
        n, m = len(str1), len(str2)
        while i < n and j < m:
            c1 = str1[i]
            c2 = str2[j]
            if c1 == c2 or chr((ord(c1) - 96) % 26 + 97) == c2:
                j += 1
            i += 1
        return j == m
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Single pass greedy matching: for each character in str1 we check if it already matches or can be incremented once to match the current character in str2. If so we advance both pointers, otherwise only the str1 pointer. If we match all of str2 we return True.
